name: Blackberry Cobbler
recipeIngredient: 4 cups Blackberries, picked over, rinsed and drained well
6 tablespoons Unsalted Butter, cold, cut into bits
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, stir together the cornstarch and 1/4 cup cold water until the cornstarch is completely dissolved.
2. Add 1 cup sugar, lemon juice, and blackberries to the cornstarch mixture. Combine gently but thoroughly.
3. Transfer the blackberry mixture to an 8-inch cast-iron skillet.
4. In a separate bowl, combine the flour, remaining sugar, baking powder, and salt. Blend in the butter until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
5. Add 1/4 cup boiling water to the flour mixture and stir until it forms a dough.
6. Bring the blackberry mixture to a boil on top of the stove, stirring.
7. Drop spoonfuls of the dough carefully onto the boiling mixture.
8. Bake the cobbler in a preheated 400°F oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown. Serve warm with vanilla ice cream.
